## Summary

Two production files had hardcoded `gpt-4o` model strings that bypassed the configured `EVAL_OPENAI_MODEL` env var and the code default of `gpt-5.1`:

- **`runPass3Preflight.ts`** — `PASS3A_DEFAULT_MODEL` was `"gpt-4o"`, now `"gpt-5.1"`
- **`polishPass.ts`** — `model:` parameter in `callPolishLLM` was `"gpt-4o"`, now `"gpt-5.1"`

Also deleted 4 Vercel env vars (`EVAL_OPENAI_MODEL`, `EVAL_PASS1_MODEL`, `EVAL_PASS2_MODEL`, `EVAL_PASS3_MODEL`) so code defaults always win — no more hidden overrides.

## Scope

- **Touches:** `lib/evaluation/pipeline/runPass3Preflight.ts`, `lib/evaluation/polishPass.ts`
- **Does NOT touch:** Tests, policy.ts token cap tables, cost.ts pricing tables (these reference old models for historical lookups only)

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- `gpt-5.1` may produce slightly different preflight/polish outputs vs `gpt-4o` — expected and acceptable since all other passes already use `gpt-5.1`
- Vercel env var deletion is already done — no code rollback will restore them

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: N/A — this changes the model used for Pass 3A preflight and polish pass from gpt-4o to gpt-5.1, reducing cost significantly
